key_insight: An autonomous LLM coding agent improved a crystal graph network for band-gap prediction and achieved the best no-pretraining result on MatBench by combining known modeling ideas in a new implementation.
engineering_relevance: This suggests LLM-driven research loops can accelerate materials-model optimization for property prediction, helping engineers rapidly refine structure-to-property predictors on standard benchmarks.
method: Built an expert-designed crystal graph network for band-gap prediction.
Added element-pair features on message-passing edges.
Included a crystal space-group embedding and other borrowed ML improvements.
evidence_signal: On the MatBench band-gap benchmark with more than 100k crystals, the agent-produced model outperformed all 17 reported expert-designed models while training without external pretraining.
watch_item: The gains appear to come from integrating known techniques rather than discovering fundamentally new methods, so transferability and novelty should be interpreted cautiously.
paper_title: Optimizing Expert-Designed Crystal Graph Networks for Band-Gap Prediction with an Autonomous LLM Research Loop
